summary: Small matching PR that replaced three easy_func placeholders with C implementations: fighter common Item Scope air fire collision handling, one Home-Run Contest ground animation helper, and one Home-Run stage float accessor/scaler. The decomp.dev report credited 3 new matches and +124 matched bytes, with each new function moving from 0% to 100%.

key_files.reason: Implemented ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll as a simple wrapper calling ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_800D8268).
Added the missing prototype for fn_800D8268(Fighter_GObj* gobj), enabling the new collision wrapper to reference it cleanly.
Implemented grHomeRun_8021DEB4 and grHomeRun_8021EF10, removing two placeholders in the Home-Run Contest ground file.

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder /// #ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll with void ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll(Fighter_GObj* gobj) { ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_800D8268); }.
Added the header declaration /* 0D8268 */ void fn_800D8268(Fighter_GObj* gobj); in ftCo_Attack100.h near neighboring ItemScope callback prototypes.
Replaced the placeholder /// #grHomeRun_8021DEB4 with a function that obtains Ground* via GET_GROUND(gobj) and calls grAnime_801C8138((HSD_GObj*) gobj, gp->map_id, 0).
Added extern f32 grHr_804DBC94; in grhomerun.c for use by grHomeRun_8021EF10.
Replaced the placeholder /// #grHomeRun_8021EF10 with f32 grHomeRun_8021EF10(void) { return grHr_804DBC94 * Ground_801C0498(); }.
decomp.dev reported 3 new matches: grHomeRun_8021DEB4 (+44 bytes), ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll (+40 bytes), and grHomeRun_8021EF10 (+40 bytes).
smart_moves: Matched ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll by recognizing it as the airborne counterpart of nearby Item Scope collision wrappers: the implementation is just a transition/collision helper call with a callback argument.
Kept the callback fn_800D8268 declared in the relevant ftCo_Attack100 header rather than relying on an undeclared symbol in the C file.

For grHomeRun_8021DEB4, used the existing GET_GROUND macro to access the Ground object, then passed gp->map_id to the stage animation helper; this follows common ground-module patterns and avoids manual struct field guessing in the call site.
For grHomeRun_8021EF10, expressed the function as a direct multiplication of an external file-scope float symbol and Ground_801C0498(), producing a compact exact match.
decomp_lessons: Very small wrapper functions are often recoverable by comparing neighboring action-state/collision functions with similar names and signatures.
When a matched function references a local callback by address, add the callback prototype to the corresponding header in address order with the surrounding declarations.
Ground module helpers commonly take a Ground_GObj*, recover Ground* using GET_GROUND, and call animation helpers such as grAnime_801C8138 with an HSD_GObj cast plus a map_id field.
Extern declarations for data symbols can be sufficient for matching scalar-return helper functions when the symbol is not yet fully typed or owned by a header.
Even low-line-count easy_func PRs can improve matching metrics when they remove placeholders in multiple translation units.
naming_conventions: Fighter common functions keep the ftCo_ prefix for named behavior functions, as in ftCo_ItemScopeAirFire_Coll.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed local helper callbacks retain fn_-address naming, e.g. fn_800D8268.
Home-Run Contest ground functions use the grHomeRun_ address-based prefix, e.g. grHomeRun_8021DEB4 and grHomeRun_8021EF10.
The external float data symbol follows the stage abbreviation plus address pattern: grHr_804DBC94.
Header declarations include address comments such as /* 0D8268 */ and are placed with nearby address-ordered prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Identify pure wrapper functions where the body is a single call with the original gobj and a callback function pointer.

Use neighboring matched functions in the same file to infer helper choice and callback signature; ftCo_ItemScopeFire_Coll already called ft_800841B8(gobj, fn_800D82EC), while the new airborne variant calls ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_800D8268).
For ground functions, preserve expected casts such as (HSD_GObj*) gobj when passing Ground_GObj* to generic HSD/animation APIs.
Model simple floating-point return functions directly as data-symbol multiplication with another helper call: grHr_804DBC94 * Ground_801C0498().
Use exact argument literals, such as the third argument 0 to grAnime_801C8138, rather than introducing named constants without evidence.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported GALE01 matched code at 46.96% with +124 bytes and 3 new 100% function matches.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find nearby ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Coll and ftCo_ItemScopeFire_Coll wrappers to infer remaining Item Scope collision helpers.
Search for other grHomeRun placeholders around grHomeRun_8021EEBC that may follow the same ground animation or scalar-return patterns.
Investigate ownership and typing of data symbol grHr_804DBC94 for possible relocation from extern-in-C into an appropriate header or data declaration.
Look for other ground modules using GET_GROUND followed by grAnime_801C8138((HSD_GObj*) gobj, gp->map_id, 0) as templates for matching simple stage init/animation functions.
